title: hibbertia stricta subsp. furcatula
intro: Hibbertia stricta subsp. furcatula, commonly known as Guinea flower, is a flowering plant species native to Australia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of skin conditions such as eczema and acne due to its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The plant is rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which are believed to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, providing various health benefits including improved cardiovascular health and reduced risk of certain cancers. Hibbertia stricta subsp. furcatula can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, allowing for its medicinal properties to be harnessed and utilized for various health purposes.
